rationale: The query's engine is an exact self-consistent equation for a critical point governed by moments of the degree distribution P(k), producing anomalous (vanishing threshold / non-standard-order transition) behavior when P(k) is heavy-tailed and its moments diverge; the hyp_abstract reframes this as a cascading-failure robustness threshold set by the ratio of mean connectivity to its second moment, again anomalous for heavy-tailed networks. The candidate ("Catastrophic cascade of failures in interdependent networks") builds an analytical self-consistent framework deriving an exact critical average-degree threshold for network collapse, and explicitly reports the same anomalous-broadness signature (a broader degree distribution flips from stabilizing to destabilizing), so the same moment-driven self-consistent threshold machinery is genuinely present. The object differs: the query's substrate is a ferromagnetic spin/magnetization system on a graph, while the candidate's substrate is functional/operational failure cascades across interdependent networks (engineering/societal systems) — a real cross-field transfer from statistical-mechanics criticality to network-robustness/cascading-failure engineering, matching the spirit of hyp_generic_object's operational-failure framing rather than the query's own spin system.
